what_it_does: This file is a metadata attachment record that links a design-source channel entry to an image asset located at image://design-source/recent-pitches/p-17 and records when the attachment was added.
observations: The file consists only of YAML frontmatter with no body content
The target field uses an image:// URI pointing to recent-pitches/p-17
It has an id att-b8ebd111 and channel design-source
It records an ISO-8601 added timestamp
standouts: References a specific image asset in the recent-pitches namespace
Serves as a typed, timestamped channel attachment ledger entry
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for structured metadata
Includes id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields
Target uses a custom image:// URI scheme
concerns: No human-readable description, caption, or title for the attachment
The image:// target cannot be verified from this file alone
No author or originating message context is recorded
notes: A bare attachment ledger entry that points a design-source channel record to an image asset and timestamps the addition.
questions: What is pitch p-17 and what does the image depict?
Who added this attachment and to which specific channel message?
Is the referenced image still present and accessible?
purpose_vs_actual: The fields imply it should record an image attachment to a channel; the content does exactly that, though with minimal context.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to catalog image attachments to the design-source channel so they can be retrieved alongside related messages or pitches.
novelty: Uses an image:// URI scheme to target an image asset rather than a filesystem path.
contribution: Provides a timestamped, typed pointer from a channel to an image asset, preserving the fact and timing of the attachment for retrieval and provenance.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-only channel attachment record with id att-b8ebd111 that links the design-source channel to image asset recent-pitches/p-17 via an image:// URI and records an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: This file was created to record that an image asset was attached to a design-source channel entry at a specific time.
